page: Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration features 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with a substantial L3 cache of 260 MB, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. The server is provisioned with 336.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core and text processing capabilities. The server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2338.0 and a text processing score of 2975.0, both placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression at 34.77 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.31 GB/sec also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into the average range, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8962.0 and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 64.79 GB/sec. For LLM workloads, the server performs well on smaller models, reaching 317.79 tokens/sec for text generation with a 135M model, but drops to a weaker 8.64 tokens/sec on a 7B model.

Given its massive memory allocation relative to its 8 vCPUs, the FX16-8ms_v2 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads. It represents a specialized hardware profile where memory capacity is prioritized over high core counts, making it highly qualitative for memory-intensive applications that do not scale linearly with vCPUs. This server is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, heavy text processing, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM alongside high single-core CPU frequency.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, featuring an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 336.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 42.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2338.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics align with average tiers, and larger LLM inference tasks show performance trade-offs. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from high single-core clock speeds, such as in-memory databases, Redis caching, and complex text processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU with 8 vCPUs, 336 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is an Intel-powered, memory-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 336 GB RAM, and high single-core clock speeds.
tagline: A memory-intensive server combining massive RAM capacity with high-frequency single-core performance for demanding enterprise databases and caching.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Massive 336.0 GB RAM allocation providing 42.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
No local storage or GPU, but compatible with Premium Storage
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and text processing
